description: Close any People CRUD planning metadata gaps so stored and proposed single-record checks can consume the shared People relationship core without inventing endpoint-specific subject models.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner produces operation-applicable check specs for all five People relationship strategies for stored GET-by-id, DELETE, PUT, and POST-as-update checks.

RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner produces proposed-value check specs for POST create-new, PUT, and POST-as-update using direct person terminal DocumentIds, existing-target self-person DocumentIds, and transitive first-hop anchors from the finalized root row.

POST create-new omits self-person root DocumentId subjects as operation-ineligible and reports a no-executable-subject security-configuration failure when a selected relationship strategy has no executable subjects after eligibility filtering.
Person subjects whose resolved paths start in child collections remain ineligible for ODS-parity CRUD authorization and retain skipped contributor diagnostics.
Missing People auth views, unresolved People paths, missing proposed root bindings, and no-applicable-subject selections remain security-configuration failures rather than authorization denials.
No-claims planning metadata includes executable People subjects, auth-view names, output columns, readable securable names, value source, and hint metadata for synthetic CRUD 403 failures.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner.cs and RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector.cs for stored/proposed People subject eligibility and anchor metadata.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector"
Inspect tests for coverage of self, direct, transitive, child-collection, no-claims, missing auth-view, and no-executable-subject People cases.

description: Remove the temporary People single-record endpoint staging block and let GET-by-id, POST, PUT, and DELETE compose People relationship checks with the existing relationship authorization result pipeline.
acceptance-criteria: TryCreatePeopleEndpointStagingFailures no longer converts People relationship strategies for GET-by-id, POST, PUT, or DELETE into temporary 501 responses.

RelationshipAuthorizationEndpointExecutionBoundary no longer rejects supported People relationship check specs for single-record SQL execution while still rejecting invalid auth-object or subject metadata.
Security-configuration failures from People planning surface as canonical security-configuration failures before execution, not as staging responses.
NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired remains a no-op when combined with People relationship strategies.

Any NamespaceBased, OwnershipBased, or custom view-based CRUD strategy that is still unimplemented in the relational path continues to fail closed through the explicit temporary not-implemented result instead of being ignored.
Mixed relationship strategies are passed as one OR group, with EdOrg and People subjects inside each strategy combined with AND semantics.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs for removal of People-specific staging in GET-by-id, POST, PUT, and DELETE authorization paths.
Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationEndpointExecutionBoundary.cs for People-aware single-record validation.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepository|FullyQualifiedName~SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor"

description: Extend SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler to emit stored-value People authorization checks for self, direct, and transitive person paths.
acceptance-criteria: Stored self Student, Contact, and Staff subjects compare the target root DocumentId to the selected People auth view output column.
Stored direct person references compare the stored terminal person DocumentId column to the selected People auth view output column.

Stored transitive person paths anchor on the target root DocumentId, walk the ordered root-to-terminal DocumentId join path, and compare the terminal person DocumentId to the selected People auth view output column.
Null direct stored person values, missing intermediate rows, and null terminal values are encoded as stored-value invalid-data failures for the original person securable element.
No matching auth-view membership is encoded as no-relationship, not invalid data.

Each configured relationship strategy emits a parenthesized single-record EXISTS check group; executable subjects inside that strategy are combined with AND and relationship strategies are combined with OR.

EdOrg subjects in mixed EdOrg-and-People strategies keep concrete root-table scope, direct-claim-match behavior, normal hierarchy direction, and inverted Source/Target hierarchy direction where applicable.

The failed OR group still produces the versioned compact AUTH1 payload with emitted check index, plan-relative strategy ordinal, subject ordinal, and runtime failure kind for every failed subject in configured order.
PostgreSQL aborts unauthorized batches with the established dms.throw_error AUTH1 pattern, and SQL Server aborts with the established CAST('AUTH1 - ...' AS INT) pattern.

PostgreSQL and SQL Server SQL use the existing ClaimEducationOrganizationIds parameterization contract, including SQL Server scalar parameters below 2,000 IDs and dms.BigIntTable at 2,000 or more IDs.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler.cs for People stored-value path rendering and failure-kind selection.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler"
Inspect generated PostgreSQL and SQL Server expectations for self, direct Student, direct Contact, direct Staff, transitive Student, responsibility Student, and mixed EdOrg-and-People checks.

description: Extend proposed-value People authorization so POST create-new, PUT, and POST-as-update authorize finalized row values before insert, If-Match, guarded no-op, or update DML.
acceptance-criteria: Proposed direct person subjects bind the finalized root-row terminal person DocumentId value that will be persisted.
Existing-resource self person subjects bind the locked existing target DocumentId for PUT and POST-as-update proposed checks.
POST create-new self person subjects are omitted as operation-ineligible and never self-bootstrap through a row that is not yet visible to People auth views.
Proposed transitive person subjects bind the finalized first-hop DocumentId anchor and resolve the terminal person DocumentId through the ordered path inside the authorization SQL.
Null proposed direct values, null first-hop anchors, missing intermediate rows, and null terminal values are encoded as proposed element-required failures for the original person securable element.
RelationalWriteNoProfilePersister no longer throws for TransitivePeopleFirstHopAnchorValue; the People-aware proposed authorization path resolves it before auth-view membership checks.
Proposed authorization uses the finalized profile-aware root row or merged rowset, including hidden stored values preserved by a writable profile, not raw JSON.
POST create-new composes proposed authorization and dms.Document insert in the same provider command or batch so AUTH1 denial leaves no persisted create artifacts.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationshipAuthorizationProposedValueExtractor.cs and RelationalWriteNoProfilePersister.cs for People proposed runtime value handling.
Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler.cs for proposed People subquery rendering.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationProposedValueExtractor|FullyQualifiedName~SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor"

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler"

description: Wire People relationship authorization into GET-by-id while preserving not-found behavior, authorization-before-hydration, and the existing observed read-boundary guard.
acceptance-criteria: GET-by-id for an existing target with a People relationship strategy authorizes stored values before hydration or readable-profile projection.
Unauthorized GET-by-id returns relationship-not-authorized 403 and does not execute reconstitution or materialization work.
A missing target still returns the existing not-found response before no-claims or relationship authorization denial.

If the relational GET-by-id flow already supports an If-None-Match or not-modified target-resolution short-circuit, that short-circuit is preserved without adding an authorization-only roundtrip afterward.
StoredDocument read mode remains an explicit internal authorization bypass; ExternalResponse mode cannot request that bypass.
GET-by-id authorization and hydration share the same observed target read boundary using the existing ContentVersion guard/retry or an equivalent mechanism.
Intermediate People path rows and auth-view membership are evaluated by the authorization statement under normal provider semantics; no additional post-authorization recheck is introduced.
No-claims GET-by-id for an existing target returns a synthetic relationship 403 with People readable names and hints.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs GET-by-id authorization flow and read-boundary retry handling.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepository"

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalGetByIdAuthorization"

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalGetByIdAuthorization" when SQL Server is available.

description: Wire People relationship authorization into DELETE under the same locked write transaction or guard used for If-Match and delete execution.
acceptance-criteria: DELETE authorizes stored People values after target existence is established and after the target Document row is locked or guarded.
Unauthorized DELETE returns relationship-not-authorized 403 before If-Match comparison, delete execution, delete constraint resolution, or use/conflict errors.
When stored authorization and If-Match would both fail for an existing target, the response is 403 rather than 412.
Unauthorized DELETE leaves dms.Document, resource rows, child rows, change-query journal rows, and referential identity rows unchanged.
No-claims DELETE for an existing target returns a synthetic relationship 403 with People readable names and hints.
Authorized DELETE preserves existing If-Match and delete conflict behavior.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs DELETE flow around target locking, authorization, If-Match, and delete execution.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepository|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDelete"

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDeleteAuthorization"

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDeleteAuthorization" when SQL Server is available.

description: Wire People relationship authorization into POST create-new so proposed People values are checked before any create artifact can persist.
acceptance-criteria: POST create-new with People relationship strategies authorizes finalized proposed values before inserting dms.Document.

Unauthorized POST create-new returns relationship-not-authorized 403 and leaves no dms.Document, resource root, child, extension, ReferentialIdentity, change-query journal, or ownership-stamped document artifacts.
Empty normalized EdOrg claims return relationship-not-authorized 403 before reference resolution, descriptor resolution, or create-vs-existing target detection.
POST create-new with If-Match returns 403 when proposed authorization fails and 412 only after proposed authorization succeeds.
Reference-resolution failures remain reference-resolution failures when the request is not already denied by empty claims or security-configuration planning.

People same-row bootstrap is not hardcoded; association and person create behavior comes from effective security metadata such as NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired, EdOrg-only primary association authorization, or StudentsOnly overrides.
POST requests that resolve to an existing resource follow the stored-then-proposed update path and no longer expose a People staging result.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs POST preflight and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs create flow.
Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWriteNoProfilePersister.cs for inline proposed authorization before document insert.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepository|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWrite"

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalPostAuthorization"

description: Wire People relationship authorization into PUT and POST-as-update with stored-value authorization first and proposed-value authorization second.
acceptance-criteria: Existing-target PUT and POST-as-update authorize stored People values before request-dependent reference resolution, profile merge work, guarded no-op success, If-Match comparison, or update DML.

Existing-target PUT and POST-as-update authorize proposed People values whenever at least one executable proposed People subject can be supplied by the finalized profile-aware rowset, even if values appear unchanged.
Stored authorization and proposed authorization each evaluate the full configured relationship OR group independently; the same strategy does not need to authorize both states.
Proposed authorization failure returns 403 before stale If-Match, guarded no-op success, or update DML can be exposed.

Stored authorization, reference outputs that feed proposed People anchors, current-state/profile merge work, proposed authorization, If-Match, guarded no-op, and update DML share a locked write transaction/session or equivalent ContentVersion guard/retry boundary.

Unauthorized stored-value, proposed-value, and no-op update attempts leave dms.Document stamps, resource rows, child rows, extension rows, ReferentialIdentity rows, and change-query journal rows unchanged.
Writable-profile PUT and POST-as-update use the finalized merged rowset, including hidden stored People anchors, for proposed authorization.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/StoredRelationshipAuthorizationOrchestrator.cs and ProposedRelationshipAuthorizationOrchestrator.cs for existing-target ordering and guarded-boundary behavior.
Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor.cs for If-Match, no-op, merge, and persist ordering.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepository|FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationProposedValueExtractor"

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalPutAuthorization"

description: Audit and update relational security metadata, ApiSchema-derived authorization inputs, claim-set fixtures, and setup data needed for ODS-effective People CRUD behavior.
acceptance-criteria: Person create operations use NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired when that is the effective ODS metadata.
Primary relationship create operations that should be EdOrg-only are not forced through same-row People relationship authorization.
StudentContactAssociation uses the RelationshipsWithStudentsOnly override for applicable CRUD operations.
StudentEducationOrganizationResponsibilityAssociation follows its relationshipBasedData metadata and through-responsibility auth view behavior.

Runtime authorization inputs carry People auth view table names, DocumentId output columns, through-responsibility selection, hints, and readable securable names from metadata rather than hardcoded endpoint exceptions.

Claim-set and integration/E2E fixture data can exercise Student, Contact, Staff, direct, self, transitive, and through-responsibility People CRUD cases without relying on unrelated authorization stories.
Any discovered non-People-CRUD relational exclusion remains untagged and is documented in the relevant test fixture or task notes rather than being silently restored.
steps-to-verify: Review ApiSchema, authorization metadata, and test fixture inputs used by src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration and src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E for People CRUD resources.

Review src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Authorization/RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndContacts.feature, RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeople.feature, RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndStaffs.feature, and RelationshipsWithStudentsOnlyThroughResponsibility.feature for fixture assumptions.

Run targeted metadata/planner unit tests with claim-set combinations that select NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired, EdOrg-only, StudentsOnly, StudentsOnlyThroughResponsibility, and mixed EdOrg-and-People strategies.

description: Add focused planner and endpoint-boundary unit coverage for People CRUD strategy selection, operation eligibility, failure metadata, and composition.
acceptance-criteria: Tests cover RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeople, RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeopleInverted, RelationshipsWithPeopleOnly, RelationshipsWithStudentsOnly, and RelationshipsWithStudentsOnlyThroughResponsibility for stored and proposed single-record planning.

Tests prove EdOrg subjects in mixed strategies use DMS concrete root-table scope and direct-claim plus hierarchy semantics, with inverted strategies swapping only EdOrg hierarchy Source/Target filtering.
Tests prove People subjects select Student, Contact, Staff, and through-responsibility auth views with the correct DocumentId output columns and hints.
Tests prove direct, self, transitive, and child-collection People paths produce the expected executable or ineligible subject metadata.

Tests prove create-new self-person ineligibility, no-executable-subject configuration failure, missing auth-view configuration failure, no-claims synthetic failure metadata, and missing proposed root binding configuration failure.

Tests prove mixed relationship strategies OR together, subjects inside a strategy AND together, duplicate configured strategies preserve ordinals, and NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired contributes no checks or hints.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner|FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationStrategyClassifier|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector|FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationEndpointExecutionBoundary"
Review new NUnit fixtures for Given_ class names, Setup arrange/act methods, and It_ assertion method names.
Confirm tests do not depend on provider databases or E2E stack setup.

description: Add focused SQL compiler, proposed-value extractor, AUTH1 payload, and provider-failure mapping unit coverage for People single-record authorization.
acceptance-criteria: SQL compiler tests cover PostgreSQL and SQL Server stored self, direct Student, direct Contact, direct Staff, transitive Student, through-responsibility Student, mixed EdOrg-and-People AND, and multiple OR strategies.

SQL compiler tests cover PostgreSQL and SQL Server proposed direct person, existing-target self person, transitive first-hop anchor, proposed element-required, stored invalid-data, no-relationship, and SQL Server scalar-vs-TVP parameterization branches.

Proposed-value extractor tests prove finalized row values, existing target DocumentId anchors, transitive first-hop runtime values, profile-hidden stored values, and null proposed values are mapped correctly.
AUTH1 payload tests prove People subject ordinals and runtime failure kinds map back to RelationshipAuthorizationFailure DTO entries without post-failure database lookups.
Malformed, unknown-version, truncated, and unmappable People AUTH1 payloads map to canonical security-configuration failures.
Generated SQL expectations do not use UniqueId or USI values for People authorization.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler|FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload|FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper"

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationProposedValueExtractor|FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationProviderFailureMapper"
Inspect generated SQL assertion names for both stored and proposed People path shapes.

description: Add repository and write-executor unit coverage for People CRUD response precedence, staging removal, read/write boundaries, and side-effect prevention.
acceptance-criteria: GET-by-id tests prove People relationship strategies no longer return the temporary 501, unauthorized requests return 403 without hydration, no-claims existing targets return 403, not-found remains not-found, and StoredDocument mode bypass is internal-only.

DELETE tests prove People relationship strategies no longer return the temporary 501, authorization runs under the locked target boundary, 403 wins over If-Match mismatch, and unauthorized deletes do not execute delete SQL.

POST create tests prove empty claims short-circuit before reference and target detection, proposed authorization runs before dms.Document insert, unauthorized creates leave no artifacts, and If-Match returns 403 before 412 when proposed authorization fails.

PUT and POST-as-update tests prove stored authorization gates request-dependent work, proposed authorization runs before If-Match and guarded no-op, and unauthorized no-op/update attempts leave persisted state unchanged.
Tests prove known unimplemented non-relationship CRUD strategies still return explicit not-implemented results and true security-configuration failures still return security-configuration results.

Tests prove relationship-not-authorized results carry full People failure DTO metadata, including value source, strategy ordinals, subject ordinals, person kind, auth view, readable names, hints, and claim context.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepository|FullyQualifiedName~SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalWrite"
Review test fakes to confirm hydration, delete, document insert, no-op, and update operations are not called after People relationship denial.
Review assertion coverage around UpdateResult, UpsertResult, DeleteResult, and GetResult relationship-not-authorized variants.

description: Add PostgreSQL backend integration coverage for People CRUD authorization using real relational persistence, auth views, and provider AUTH1 behavior.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL tests cover authorized and unauthorized GET-by-id with no reconstitution on denial.
PostgreSQL tests cover authorized and unauthorized POST create-new with no persisted create artifacts on denial.
PostgreSQL tests cover PUT or POST-as-update stored-value denial, proposed-value denial, unauthorized no-op denial, and unchanged persisted state.
PostgreSQL tests cover authorized and unauthorized DELETE with no delete on denial and 403-over-412 precedence.

Across the PostgreSQL People CRUD tests, coverage includes Student, Contact, Staff, through-responsibility Student, one self/direct path, one transitive path, empty EdOrg claims, missing People auth view configuration failure, SQL-seeded stored invalid data, proposed element required, and mixed EdOrg-and-People cases where EdOrg passes/People fails, People passes/EdOrg fails, and a second OR strategy authorizes.
Tests assert persisted side effects for unauthorized writes and deletes, including dms.Document, resource rows, child rows, ReferentialIdentity, and change-query journal rows where applicable.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~PeopleRelationship|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalGetByIdAuthorization|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalPostAuthorization|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalPutAuthorization|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDeleteAuthorization"

Run dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "Category=PostgresqlIntegration&FullyQualifiedName~PeopleRelationship"
Inspect PostgreSQL test setup to ensure direct SQL seeding is used only for invalid stored-data or setup cases that normal API validation cannot create.

description: Add SQL Server backend integration coverage for People CRUD authorization using real relational persistence, auth views, provider AUTH1 behavior, and ClaimEducationOrganizationIds parameterization thresholds.
acceptance-criteria: SQL Server tests cover authorized and unauthorized GET-by-id with no reconstitution on denial.
SQL Server tests cover authorized and unauthorized POST create-new with no persisted create artifacts on denial.
SQL Server tests cover PUT or POST-as-update stored-value denial, proposed-value denial, unauthorized no-op denial, and unchanged persisted state.
SQL Server tests cover authorized and unauthorized DELETE with no delete on denial and 403-over-412 precedence.

Across the SQL Server People CRUD tests, coverage includes Student, Contact, Staff, through-responsibility Student, one self/direct path, one transitive path, empty EdOrg claims, missing People auth view configuration failure, SQL-seeded stored invalid data, proposed element required, and mixed EdOrg-and-People cases where EdOrg passes/People fails, People passes/EdOrg fails, and a second OR strategy authorizes.
SQL Server tests prove expanded scalar ClaimEducationOrganizationIds are used below 2,000 unique IDs and dms.BigIntTable is used at 2,000 or more unique IDs after dedupe/sort.
steps-to-verify: Run ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin='<admin connection string>' dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~PeopleRelationship|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalGetByIdAuthorization|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalPostAuthorization|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalPutAuthorization|FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDeleteAuthorization"

Run ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin='<admin connection string>' dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "Category=MssqlIntegration&FullyQualifiedName~PeopleRelationship"
Inspect captured SQL or parameter metadata assertions for SQL Server scalar and TVP branches.

description: Add People CRUD ProblemDetails integration coverage by feeding People relationship failures through the shared relationship failure parser and formatter.
acceptance-criteria: Unauthorized People GET-by-id, POST create, PUT or POST-as-update, and DELETE responses return 403 ProblemDetails matching auth.md structure with type, title, detail, errors, status, and correlationId.
ProblemDetails tests cover People no-relationship, no-claims rendered as none, stored invalid-data, proposed element-required, and mixed failure-kind precedence.
Readable securable names prefer carried MetaEd/readable names and fall back from JSON paths when needed.
ProblemDetails tests cover singular and plural securable-element wording and EdOrg claim rendering with at most five displayed claims followed by ... when claims are truncated.
Student, Contact, Staff, and through-responsibility hints are appended and de-duplicated in deterministic configured strategy order.

When multiple OR strategies fail, all failed People strategies and failed subjects are preserved internally and the user-facing errors aggregate readable names according to the shared formatter rules.
Malformed or unmappable People AUTH1 payloads return canonical security-configuration 500 responses rather than generic system errors or fallback 403 responses.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Scenarios/RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetailsScenario.cs for People endpoint-level cases.

Run dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~RelationshipAuthorizationProblemDetails|FullyQualifiedName~PeopleRelationship"
Validate ProblemDetails assertions treat correlationId as present/non-empty unless the test intentionally supplies a fixed correlation-id header.

description: Revalidate relational E2E exclusions and restore only scenarios whose remaining blocker is People GET-by-id, POST, PUT, or DELETE authorization.
acceptance-criteria: Current relational exclusions are revalidated before retagging; scenarios are not restored solely because they appeared in earlier acceptance lists.
Eligible restored scenarios receive @relational-backend and an appropriate @relational-ci-shard-* tag.

Expected restore candidates from EducationOrganizationChanges, RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndContacts, RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeople, DisciplineActionAuthorization, OrganizationDepartmentAuthorization, RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndStaffs, and RelationshipsWithStudentsOnlyThroughResponsibility are restored only when their setup and assertions are People CRUD-dependent.
TokenIntrospection scenarios 02 and 03 remain excluded unless revalidation proves their relational exclusion is caused by People CRUD setup.

StudentAssessmentRegistrationBatteryPartAssociation scenario 01 remains excluded unless revalidation proves its remaining blocker is People CRUD authorization rather than namespace, reference, profile, or unrelated relational behavior.

Restored E2E coverage includes at least one authorized or unauthorized stored-value read or delete, one authorized or unauthorized proposed-value create, one update path, one Contact path, one Staff path, and one through-responsibility path through real claim-set/token wiring.
No People GET-many-only retagging from the completed DMS-1095 slice is changed unless required by the current CRUD restoration.
steps-to-verify: Review relational tags in src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Authorization/EducationOrganizationChanges.feature, RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndContacts.feature, RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeople.feature, RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndStaffs.feature, RelationshipsWithStudentsOnlyThroughResponsibility.feature, and RoleNamedSecurity feature files.
Run pwsh ./teardown-local-dms.ps1 from src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E.
Run pwsh ./setup-local-dms.ps1 -EnvironmentFile ./.env.e2e.relational from src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E.

Verify docker inspect dms-local-dms-1 --format '{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}' | rg 'UseRelationalBackend|Datastore|QueryHandler' includes AppSettings__UseRelationalBackend=true, AppSettings__Datastore=postgresql, and AppSettings__QueryHandler=postgresql.
Run env -u NODE_OPTIONS dotnet test EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E.csproj --configuration Release --filter "Category=relational-backend" from src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E.

description: Medium: Use create-new People relationship authorization planning for POST create-new instead of reusing existing-resource update planning.
acceptance-criteria: POST create-new proposed relationship authorization uses ProposedValueOperationKind.CreateNew semantics, including operation eligibility filtering.

Self Student, Contact, or Staff subjects selected by a create-new People relationship strategy are omitted as SelfPersonDocumentIdUnavailableForCreateNew; if no executable subjects remain, POST returns the canonical no-executable-subject security-configuration failure before reference resolution or persistence.
POST-as-update continues to use existing-resource stored-then-proposed planning, and existing-target self-person subjects bind to the locked target DocumentId.
POST create-new no longer reaches RelationshipAuthorizationProposedValueExtractor with an ExistingTargetDocumentId self-person proposed anchor.
Focused repository or executor unit coverage proves create-new self-person relationship strategies use the create-new plan, while POST-as-update self-person strategies keep the existing-target plan.
